spec.reasoning: I chose lexical renaming and a distractor column. For lexical changes: (1) Column renames: ITEM→PRODUCT, BRANDS→LABEL, BATCH→LOT, ORIGIN→SOURCE. These maintain the same descriptiveness level (single full words). I did NOT rename TYPE or DATE because 'TYPE' appears naturally in the instruction text ('TYPE' column is referenced contextually) and DATE is a very common word. I renamed ORIGIN→SOURCE in both sheets to preserve the alignment relationship. (2) Sheet renames: INPUT→DATA_IN, OUPUT→DATA_OUT, both abbreviation-style names at the same descriptiveness level. (3) Distractor column: Added 'REGION' to the input sheet after the last column — it's plausibly similar to SOURCE/ORIGIN but clearly different, testing whether the agent incorrectly uses it. No data_value_renames needed since no header names appear as data values in the cells.
